reasoning: The most recent value for AAA10Y on Nov 7, 2025, is 1.16, up significantly from around 1.01-1.05 in late October. Historically, corporate bond spreads exhibit mean reversion after sharp spikes. The FRED statistical tool predicts a ~34% chance of the spread being higher than 1.16 by Nov 16 due to this mean-reverting tendency. As the forecast horizon extends, uncertainty increases and the probability approaches 0.5. Therefore, I estimate lower short-term probabilities (mean reversion kicking in) that gradually increase towards a coin flip for distant dates, following standard random walk with mean-reversion behavior combined with expanding variance over time.
